rationale: The cited paper is used only as part of a grouped background citation when the authors note prior work on functional versus hedonic trade-offs. The citing paper does not discuss this specific paper's core findings in detail, does not derive hypotheses from it, and does not reuse its methods or measures. The article's main theoretical development and evidence center on health-claim-induced goal conflict, supported by other directly discussed sources. So this citation serves as general literature context rather than a distinct substantive dependency.
